headline: Visa Expands Stablecoin Cards Testing Blockchain Rails And Valuation Story
summary: Visa (NYSE:V) is expanding its stablecoin backed card program through a partnership with Bridge, a Stripe company. The program is now live in 18 countries, with an ambition to reach more than 100 countries by the end of the year. Visa and Bridge are also piloting on chain settlement, allowing card transactions to be settled in stablecoins over supported blockchains. The rollout connects to crypto platforms such as MetaMask and Phantom, enabling more stablecoin holders to spend at Visa...

headline: Inside the Quest at Colossus to Replace Visa and Mastercard With KYC-Less Crypto Cards
summary: With a “box of goodies” and a team of four, the firm is trying to replace payment incumbents with an Ethereum layer-2

headline: Why The Visa (V) Investment Story Is Subtly Shifting After Fresh Analyst Updates
summary: Visa’s fair value estimate has been nudged from US$400.81 to about US$400.20 per share, a small adjustment that still keeps the stock near the US$400 mark that features in several Street price targets. That tweak lines up with recent research that combines long term optimism on Visa’s role in payments with some caution around valuation and regulation, which is prompting only modest target trims rather than wholesale rerating. As you read on, you will see how to interpret these shifts and...

headline: Chamath Palihapitiya Says Elon Musk's 'X Money' Could Turn Social Profiles Into 'Crucial Financial Asset'
summary: Silicon Valley investor Chamath Palihapitiya is signaling a massive shift in the global financial landscape, claiming that the integration of “X Money” will transform the social platform into a dominant financial system. The End Of Traditional Banking? The commentary follows...

headline: Stablecoin firms bet big on AI agent payments that barely exist
summary: Two weeks ago, a Citrini Research scenario imagining AI agents routing around card network fees sent Visa Inc., Mastercard Inc. and American Express Co. shares tumbling as much as 5% in a single session.  On its Feb. 25 earnings call, Circle Chief Executive Officer Jeremy Allaire argued that stablecoins could become the native currency of machine-to-machine commerce.
